response.justification: Strong software engineering fundamentals and extensive seniority, but C++ expertise is outdated (15-year gap from active work) and entirely lacks industrial automation, cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443), and domain-specific protocol experience critical to this role. Could be retrained in C++ and industrial systems, but represents significant ramp-up time compared to ideal candidates.
strengths: Extensive seniority (20+ years, current staff-level engineer) and multi-language expertise across systems development
Demonstrated software craftsmanship, TDD discipline, and mentoring capability across multiple organizations and roles
Proven distributed systems and high-performance design expertise (microservices, low-latency systems, millions of requests/second)
concerns: C++ skills are likely rusty; last significant C++ work was 2007–2011 (15+ years ago); recent 8 years focused exclusively on TypeScript, JavaScript, Node.js, and React
No background in industrial automation, IoT systems, or industrial cybersecurity; unfamiliar with IEC 62443 standards, SDL practices, and domain-specific protocols (Modbus, IEC 61850, OPC UA, etc.)
Recent career trajectory diverges significantly from role requirements—currently focused on web technologies and AI-agent infrastructure, not embedded systems or on-premises industrial solutions
